nodes.label: ADR-0005: Project is an unindexed, denormalized string
nodes.norm_label: adr-0005: project is an unindexed, denormalized string

nodes.norm_label: adr-0006: seed import on empty db

nodes.label: NOW layout: top-3 live, rest folded
nodes.norm_label: now layout: top-3 live, rest folded

nodes.label: In-memory source-map identity
nodes.rationale: parseVault mints a fresh id per read, so Task.id is meaningful only within one list()->mutation window, which useTasks guarantees by refreshing after every mutation. A snapshot Map<id,{path,rawLine}> matches the exact verbatim rawLine on write; match-count != 1 throws and forces refresh. Avoids polluting hand-authored Obsidian markdown with durable id:: UUIDs to solve a problem the refresh-after-mutate loop already solves for a single user.

nodes.label: Single-line splice
nodes.rationale: parseVault is lossy (drops frontmatter, headers, prose, blank lines, non-task bullets), so reconstructing a file from Task[] would delete all of that inside the owner's notes. Rule: read raw content, split by newline, replace/insert/remove exactly the one target line, rejoin, write; every other byte preserved. The load-bearing integrity decision of the slice.

nodes.label: Local-authoritative commit
nodes.rationale: Each mutation edits the file in lightning-FS then git.add + git.commit locally, which always succeeds offline on the full local repo; the mutation resolves here. Local commit is authoritative.

nodes.label: Best-effort push
nodes.rationale: After the local commit, git.push is attempted; on failure (offline/non-ff) it is swallowed and the commit stays local. Unpushed local commits ARE the queue (git's native model, no separate queue infra); push is retried opportunistically at the next mutation and next list()/on-focus refresh. No auto-merge in S15.

nodes.label: Wipe-reclone data-loss hazard
nodes.rationale: GitTransport.readFiles() did git.pull fast-forward-only else wipe-and-reclone the FS. Once S15 has unpushed local commits, a wipe-reclone destroys committed offline writes (silent data loss). Fix: push pending commits first and only wipe-reclone when the local repo has no commits ahead of origin. Both grill voices flagged it independently.

nodes.label: Inbox home
nodes.rationale: Domain-less/project-less tasks land in a top-level Inbox/ folder and Inbox.md files. parseVault maps filename Inbox (case-insensitive) to project=undefined, keeping the derived-Inbox contract without inventing a real project named Inbox. readFiles() must also scan the top-level Inbox/ folder so domain-less writes are readable on next list().

nodes.label: Git-as-transport
nodes.rationale: The PWA clones the vault git repo into local storage (isomorphic-git over a virtual FS in IndexedDB, fronted by a CORS proxy) and reads markdown from that local replica. Chosen over a bridge service and File System Access API because offline-on-Android-away-from-home is a hard requirement the bridge fails on mobile and FS Access API is Chromium-desktop-only. The same clone/commit/push mechanism becomes the S15 write path, so one transport serves both slices.

nodes.label: Promise-chain write-queue
nodes.rationale: lightning-FS/isomorphic-git on one FS is not concurrent-safe. VaultSync holds a private queue Promise; every mutation chains this.queue = this.queue.then(work) for strict FIFO single-writer serialization, one git op at a time, no library (~5 lines).

nodes.label: Durable id:: identity
nodes.rationale: Upgrade path: stamp a durable id:: into written lines (parser reads it when present, mints on first write) when a second live mutator exists. Deferred to the S16 Telegram bot editing tasks it did not author in-session, which makes in-memory session identity insufficient. That slice pays for id::, not S15.

nodes.label: rankNow balance brain
nodes.rationale: Pure rankNow(tasks, warmth, opts?) upgrades the flat priority sort to prevent one domain flooding NOW (per-domain cap ~2, inbox uncapped) and to rescue neglected domains (one rescue task from the coldest cold/stale domain). warmth is injected; rankNow never calls Date.now() or computeWarmth, preserving the pure ranking seam from ADR-0007.

nodes.label: S16 Telegram bot
nodes.rationale: Group E slice (text to create): Telegram text -> Claude intent/extract -> vault write, reusing S15's serializeTaskLine + GitTransport.writeFile. Uses Claude NLU, not the regex parseCapture (do not couple them). First second-live-mutator, so durable id:: identity lands here. HUMAN GATE (grill + PRD + ADR) before dispatch.

nodes.norm_label: s28: mission dot-tap completes task, warms domain

nodes.label: Intent-Router Seam (self-registering handler modules)
nodes.rationale: One file per intent under services/bot/intents/, each self-registering into a Map via a single append-only import line in index.ts. Chosen over a shared object-literal registry so each slice's edit isolates to its own line at the file end (rebases cleanly); filesystem dynamic-import discovery rejected as needless build-time indirection for a 4-intent bot.

nodes.label: Durable id:: Task Identity
nodes.rationale: parseTaskLine gains optional id::; serializeTaskLine always emits it; legacy id-less lines mint a UUID lazily on first write (no bulk migration). Landed at S16 per ADR-0010 §2's own upgrade trigger (a second live mutator makes in-memory session identity insufficient); a full vault-walk migration rejected as needless churn on hand-authored notes.

nodes.label: services/bot/router.ts (message-ingest dispatch layer)
nodes.rationale: Cross-pipeline hotspot: S17 (confirm gate), S18 (voice branch), S19b (photo branch + confirm-check) all insert small additive early-in-function edits; they don't logically conflict but must serialize/rebase since they land from sibling branches.

nodes.label: claude-sonnet-5 Bot NLU Model Pin
nodes.rationale: Pinned for single-turn classification+extraction on a personal low-volume bot via structured output (output_config.format), never prefill. Opus-tier strictly more accurate but not warranted; reused by S19 vision extraction.

nodes.label: Confirm-Destructive Trust Model
nodes.rationale: The bot never mutates a task the user didn't clearly ask it to touch: update/delete echo the intended change and wait for an explicit y/n; create fires instantly. Reversible, bounded, expiring pending state (in-memory Map, lazy TTL). Underlies why voice low-confidence transcripts are refused (S18) and photo bulk-create is batch-confirmed (S19).

nodes.label: Groq Whisper Transcription (whisper-large-v3-turbo)
nodes.rationale: OGG/Opus passed straight through (no ffmpeg transcoding), behind a Transcriber interface; never throws (maps errors to confident:false); confidence gate on mean no_speech_prob <= 0.5. Chosen over OpenAI Whisper for cost/latency and over local whisper.cpp to avoid heavy compute in the Node worker.

nodes.label: Vault-as-Bus Microkernel Architecture
nodes.rationale: The vault is the bus: core = vault + PWA + balance brain; integrations and agents are plugins whose only contract is markdown files. Plugins never call each other. Path-partitioning gives every writer a disjoint file set so git auto-merges with no write conflicts by construction. Fixture-first: every UI slice tests against committed fixture markdown, zero network/secrets.

nodes.label: Triple-Green Merge Gate (CI + Review + Eval)
nodes.rationale: Every v2 slice PR merges only on CI green, ponytail-review green, and a fresh read-only eval subagent verifying each numbered Definition of Done against the diff plus write-set and design-language conformance. FAIL blocks merge; 2nd FAIL escalates the implementer one model tier.

nodes.label: S50 — daily-brief agent + Home surface
nodes.rationale: GH Actions morning agent reads the whole vault and Claude-composes a strict 5-line brief; Home surfaces it dim in morning mode only. Owns Briefs/** and writes with its own scoped PAT to keep blast radius small.

nodes.label: S51 — Telegram bot logs runs to Health
nodes.rationale: Always-on bot has different cadence semantics from cron agents, so it heartbeats status.json every 15min instead of per-run; logging failures must never break message handling (error swallowed + console.warn).
nodes.norm_label: s51 — telegram bot logs runs to health

nodes.label: S52 — Supervisor report + proposal contracts + parsers
nodes.rationale: setProposalStatus is a surgical byte-preserving line flip (only the status line changes) so approval edits stay auditable; invalid status defaults to pending as a safe default.
nodes.norm_label: s52 — supervisor report + proposal contracts + parsers

nodes.label: S53 — Supervisor report card on Agents tab
nodes.norm_label: s53 — supervisor report card on agents tab

nodes.label: S54 — Proposal approval flow (owner-gated)
nodes.rationale: Human gate on agent self-modification: two-step confirm before committing the status flip (confirm-destructive spirit); agents only ever act on approved proposals. No direct fs/git in components — writes go through the transport seam.

nodes.label: S55 — supervisor agent (weekly audit to report + proposals)
nodes.rationale: Supervisor NEVER edits agent prompts/config itself — proposals-only invariant enforced in code (status hardcoded pending; no path can emit approved). Claude accuracy sampling is mockable and capped (N=15) to bound cost.
nodes.norm_label: s55 — supervisor agent (weekly audit to report + proposals)

nodes.label: S56 — Git-history guard: shallow depth + log rotation
nodes.rationale: Constant agent commits grow vault history and slow the browser clone; guards keep the clone shallow (depth:1 singleBranch) and rotate append-only files monthly. Rotation must be idempotent and lose zero lines.
nodes.norm_label: s56 — git-history guard: shallow depth + log rotation

nodes.label: S57 — Per-agent scoped tokens + shared push wrapper
nodes.rationale: Every agent gets its own fine-grained PAT so one can be revoked without touching the rest; all pushes go through one wrapper that pull --rebase → push → retries rejects with jittered backoff to survive concurrent writers.
nodes.norm_label: s57 — per-agent scoped tokens + shared push wrapper

nodes.label: LifeOS Telegram bot (services/bot)
nodes.rationale: Long-poll worker (getUpdates), not a webhook, to keep the local git clone warm across messages and avoid securing a public HTTPS endpoint for a single-owner bot; distinct BOT_VAULT_PAT from the PWA so the two surfaces rotate/revoke independently.
